perturbation.original_text: Neither the Receiving Party nor any of its agents shall, in any manner access, contact, solicit or conduct any business with a Business Source that has been made available by and through Monnit. The Receiving Party shall not in any way whatsoever circumvent or attempt to circumvent Monnit and Page 1 of 4 shall not enter into direct or indirect offers, negotiations or transactions with a Business Source revealed by Monnit.
perturbation.changed_text: Neither the Receiving Party nor any of its agents shall, in any manner access, contact, solicit or conduct any business with a Business Source that has been made available by and through Monnit, except as explicitly agreed upon in writing by both parties. The Receiving Party shall not in any way whatsoever circumvent or attempt to circumvent Monnit and Page 1 of 4 shall not enter into direct or indirect offers, negotiations or transactions with a Business Source revealed by Monnit.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an exception to the non-solicitation clause. The original text strictly prohibits any contact, solicitation or conduct of business with a Business Source provided by Monnit. The modified text allows such contact if 'explicitly agreed upon in writing'. This creates an in-text contradiction because Section 9 states that 'This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the parties and supersedes all prior proposals, representations, negotiations and communications oral or written, with respect to the subject matter herein'. If any part of the agreement can be modified, altered, or changed with only a written agreement, then section 9 is contradicted by allowing future written alterations
perturbation.contradicted_text: 9. Entire Agreement . This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the parties and supersedes all prior proposals, representations, negotiations and communications oral or written, with respect to the subject matter herein. Page 2 of 4
perturbation.justification: The modified text introduces an exception to the non-solicitation clause, allowing contact with a Business Source if 'explicitly agreed upon in writing'. This directly contradicts Section 9, the 'Entire Agreement' clause, which states that the agreement supersedes all prior or contemporaneous communications. The change implies that future written agreements can modify the original agreement, conflicting with the 'entire agreement' clause. This aligns with the definition of 'Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: The parties agree that Monnit is acting solely as a broker in bringing interested parties together to conduct business. As such, Monnit shall be entitled to compensation for such business. Compensation shall be negotiated separately and through a subsequent agreement by the Receiving Party and Monnit. Both parties agree that any business transaction(s) between the Receiving Party and those Business Sources provided by Monnit will not be conducted without the prior agreement of Monnit’s compensation for such business transaction(s).
perturbation.changed_text: The parties agree that Monnit is acting solely as a broker in bringing interested parties together to conduct business. As such, Monnit shall be entitled to compensation for such business, provided that the Receiving Party deems the services of Monnit valuable. Compensation shall be negotiated separately and through a subsequent agreement by the Receiving Party and Monnit. Both parties agree that any business transaction(s) between the Receiving Party and those Business Sources provided by Monnit will not be conducted without the prior agreement of Monnit’s compensation for such business transaction(s).
perturbation.explanation: The addition of 'provided that the Receiving Party deems the services of Monnit valuable' introduces a subjective element to Monnit's entitlement to compensation. This contradicts the statement that Monnit 'shall be entitled to compensation'. This adds ambiguity, as section 3 says that Monnit shall be entitled to compensation for such business, but if the receiving party finds the services not valuable, then Monnit is not entitled to compensation, leading to in-text contradictions.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. Broker Relationship . The parties agree that Monnit is acting solely as a broker in bringing interested parties together to conduct business. As such, Monnit shall be entitled to compensation for such business, provided that the Receiving Party deems the services of Monnit valuable. Compensation shall be negotiated separately and through a subsequent agreement by the Receiving Party and Monnit. Both parties agree that any business transaction(s) between the Receiving Party and those Business Sources provided by Monnit will not be conducted without the prior agreement of Monnit’s compensation for such business transaction(s).
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds the condition 'provided that the Receiving Party deems the services of Monnit valuable' to Monnit's entitlement to compensation. This introduces a subjective element, directly contradicting the original statement that Monnit 'shall be entitled to compensation'. The contradicted text repeats the changed text, thus maintaining the contradiction. This creates a strong in-text contradiction because the Receiving Party's subjective assessment now determines Monnit's compensation, whereas before, Monnit was unconditionally entitled to it, according to the original agreement. This is directly within the definition of Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: In the event of circumvention by any of the undersigned Parties, whether direct and/or indirect, the circumvented Party shall be entitled to a legal monetary compensation equal to ten times the maximum service fees it would have realized from such a transaction, plus any and all expenses, including any and all legal fees incurred in lieu of the recovery of such compensation.
perturbation.changed_text: In the event of circumvention by any of the undersigned Parties, whether direct and/or indirect, the circumvented Party may be entitled to a legal monetary compensation equal to up to ten times the maximum service fees it would have realized from such a transaction, plus reasonable expenses, potentially including legal fees, at the discretion of an arbitrator. Any claims of circumvention are subject to mandatory binding arbitration in Utah.
perturbation.explanation: The original text guarantees a specific compensation (ten times the service fees plus all expenses). The modified text changes 'shall be entitled' to 'may be entitled to up to ten times', introducing uncertainty. It also limits expenses to 'reasonable expenses' and adds that the discretion of an arbitrator decides the legal fees awarded. The original contract has set specific damages, but with the use of 'may be entitled' introduces ambiguity.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. Damages . In the event of circumvention by any of the undersigned Parties, whether direct and/or indirect, the circumvented Party may be entitled to a legal monetary compensation equal to up to ten times the maximum service fees it would have realized from such a transaction, plus reasonable expenses, potentially including legal fees, at the discretion of an arbitrator. Any claims of circumvention are subject to mandatory binding arbitration in Utah.
perturbation.justification: The original text guaranteed a specific compensation ('shall be entitled to a legal monetary compensation equal to ten times the maximum service fees'), while the changed text introduces uncertainty ('may be entitled to up to ten times'). This change contradicts the contradicted text, which outlines the same uncertainty, meaning that if the original text was in place, there wouldn't be any contradiction since it guarantees compensation. Since the changed text changes the conditions of payment, this creates a clear conflict regarding compensation terms. The changed text therefore creates an in-text contradiction.
